message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>SARS-CoV-2 wastewater epidemiology suffers from uncertainties concerning sample storage. We show the effect of storage of wastewater on the detectable SARS-CoV-2 load. Storage at 4 °C up to 9 days had no significant effect, while storage at −20 °C led to a significant reduction in gene copy numbers.</jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Highlights</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Raw wastewater samples can be stored up to 9 days at 4°C for SARS-CoV-2 wastewater surveillance</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Freezing of wastewater sample dramatically decreases RT-qPCR signal of SARS-CoV-2 from wastewater.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: Detection and stability of SARS-CoV-2 fragments in wastewater: Impact of storage temperature
